title: Fuel-Efficient & Affordable: Your Guide to Budget Cars with Best MPG in the UK
metaDescription: Cut fuel costs with our top picks! Discover the best budget cars with great MPG in the UK. Save money, drive smarter. Find yours at BuyAndScrap.com today.
content:
Finding **budget cars with best MPG** in the UK is crucial for saving money. The most fuel-efficient budget options typically include superminis and small hatchbacks. Look for models like the Ford Fiesta 1.0 EcoBoost, Vauxhall Corsa 1.2, or Toyota Yaris Hybrid. These cars offer low running costs, decent reliability, and excellent fuel economy. They are ideal for reducing your petrol or diesel expenses. 

## Why Fuel Efficiency Matters for Your Wallet

Fuel efficiency is vital when buying a budget car. It directly cuts your running costs. A car with better Miles Per Gallon (MPG) uses less fuel for every mile driven. Over time, these savings add up significantly. You could save hundreds, even thousands, of pounds each year. This means more money stays in your pocket, not in the fuel tank. Less fuel use also means fewer trips to the petrol station.

## What Makes a Budget Car Fuel-Efficient?

When searching for **budget cars with best MPG**, certain features make a big difference. Knowing these helps you pick the right vehicle.

### Engine Size

Smaller engines are usually more economical. Look for 1.0L to 1.6L petrol or small diesel engines. Modern engines, like turbocharged ones, offer good power and great MPG.

### Fuel Type

For long motorway journeys, a modern diesel can be very efficient. For city driving and shorter trips, petrol or a petrol-hybrid often works best.

### Vehicle Weight

Lighter cars need less energy to move. This is why city cars and superminis often lead the MPG charts.

### Gearbox

Manual gearboxes often give slightly better MPG. However, newer automatic transmissions are catching up fast.

### Maintenance

A well-kept car runs more efficiently. Always check the service history. A recent MOT certificate also confirms roadworthiness, which you can check with our [Free MOT Checker](/mot-checker).

## Our Top Picks: Budget Cars with Best MPG in the UK

Here are some excellent choices for **budget cars with best MPG**. These models consistently offer great fuel economy without costing a fortune.

### Ford Fiesta (1.0 EcoBoost / 1.5 TDCi)

A true UK legend. The Fiesta drives brilliantly and is very practical. Its 1.0-litre EcoBoost petrol and 1.5-litre TDCi diesel versions are incredibly fuel-efficient. Expect 50+ MPG from the EcoBoost and over 60 MPG from the diesel. It's a top choice for saving money.

### Vauxhall Corsa (1.2 Petrol / 1.3 CDTi)

Another supermini favourite on British roads. The Corsa is affordable to buy and run. The 1.2-litre petrol and 1.3-litre CDTi diesel engines deliver strong MPG figures. Many owners achieve over 50 MPG, especially with careful driving.

### Toyota Yaris (Hybrid)

The Yaris is known for its reliability and excellent hybrid technology. Even older models offer fantastic fuel economy. Its hybrid system is perfect for urban driving, regularly achieving 60-70 MPG. This makes it incredibly cheap to run.

### Volkswagen Polo (1.0 TSI / 1.4 TDI)

The Polo offers a more premium feel in a compact package. It shares many components with the Skoda Fabia and SEAT Ibiza. Look for the 1.0-litre TSI petrol or 1.4-litre TDI diesel engines. These versions deliver impressive MPG, often exceeding 55 MPG.

### Dacia Sandero (1.0 SCe / TCe)

The Sandero is one of the cheapest new cars available. Used models offer incredible value. It's basic but reliable, and its small engines are very frugal. You can easily see 45-55 MPG, making it a fantastic budget option.

## Beyond MPG: Other Savings with Budget Cars

Fuel efficiency is key, but budget cars offer more savings. They often fall into lower insurance groups. This means cheaper car insurance premiums for you. Road tax is also typically lower for economical vehicles. Some older, very low-emission models even qualify for free road tax. Parts and maintenance can also be more affordable. This is common for popular superminis.

## FAQs about Budget Cars with Best MPG

**Q: What is a good MPG for a budget car?**
A: A good MPG for a budget car is generally 50 MPG or higher. For smaller petrol cars, 45-55 MPG is excellent. Diesels and hybrids can often achieve 60-70 MPG.

**Q: Are older cars still fuel-efficient?**
A: Yes, many older budget cars can be very fuel-efficient. Modern smaller engines are more advanced. However, a well-maintained older car with a smaller engine can still offer great MPG. Always check its service history and get an [MOT check](/mot-checker).

**Q: Should I buy petrol or diesel for best MPG?**
A: For mostly city driving and short trips, petrol or hybrid is often better. Diesels excel on longer motorway journeys, offering higher MPG figures. Consider your typical driving needs.

**Q: How can I improve my car's MPG?**
A: Drive smoothly, avoid harsh acceleration and braking. Keep your tyres correctly inflated. Remove any unnecessary weight from your car. Ensure your car is regularly serviced. Proper maintenance can significantly boost your MPG.

Finding a great value, fuel-efficient car doesn't have to be a chore. At BuyAndScrap.com, we feature a wide range of honest, affordable cars ready for a new owner. Ready to find your next economical ride?
